records.ety: [Cf. G. dändeln to trifly, dandle, OD. & Prov. G. danten, G. tand trifly, prattle; Scot. dandill, dander, to go about idly, to trifly.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Dandled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Dandling (?).]

defs: To move up and down on one's knee or in one's arms, in affectionate play, as an infant.
quotes: Ye shall be dandled . . . upon her knees.

defs: To treat with fondness, as if a child; to fondle; to toy with; to pet.
quotes: They have put me in a silk gown and gaudy fool's cap; I as ashamed to be dandled thus.
The book, thus dandled into popularity by bishops and good ladies, contained many pieces of nursery eloquence.

defs: To play with; to put off or delay by trifles; to wheedle.
quotes: Captains do so dandle their doings, and dally in the service, as it they would not have the enemy subdued.
